purpose: Machine-readable inventory of every Python file that can directly call Microsoft Graph send/reply endpoints. Update this file whenever a Graph send surface is added, removed, or moved.

sendSurfaces.trigger: Queued Firestore outbox items for campaign launch, dashboard replies, new-property outreach, dead-letter requeue, and reviewed tour invites.
sendSurfaces.risk: This is the primary production send path; a bypass here can send placeholders, wrong recipients, duplicate retries, or paused-client messages.
sendSurfaces.nextGate: Centralize these controls into a shared outbound policy object and require every other send surface to call it or document why it is legacy-disabled.

sendSurfaces.trigger: Broker inbox reply processing that may send a same-thread response after AI classification/extraction.
sendSurfaces.risk: This is the path most likely to auto-reply from a broker response; it must never create tour scheduling, placeholders, or wrong reply-all recipients for normal users.
sendSurfaces.nextGate: Route every processing reply through the same outbound policy used by outbox sends and keep tour interpretation separate from core event handling.

sendSurfaces.trigger: Scheduled follow-up replies for active campaign threads.
sendSurfaces.risk: Follow-ups can annoy brokers or double-send if they ignore inbound replies, manual user replies, stops, opt-outs, or off-hours rules.
sendSurfaces.nextGate: Add an explicit outbound policy call before Graph send and a fixture proving manual Sent Items continuation suppresses follow-up.

sendSurfaces.trigger: Older helper functions for completion/remaining-question/new-property replies used by processing-era code paths.
sendSurfaces.risk: Helper-level Graph sends are easy to miss during safety reviews and may not share the latest placeholder, reply-all, or feature-lane checks.
currentControls: disabled by default unless SITESIFT_ENABLE_LEGACY_EMAIL_OPERATIONS=1 is explicitly set
focused regression test confirms helpers raise before Graph by default
sendSurfaces.nextGate: Delete the legacy helpers or migrate any proven caller into email_automation/email.py before enabling this lane again.

sendSurfaces.trigger: Low-level Microsoft Graph provider methods used by higher-level sending code.
sendSurfaces.risk: A provider abstraction can become a bypass if product logic calls it directly without policy checks.
currentControls: provider returns Graph identity for auditing
callers are expected to enforce policy
sendSurfaces.nextGate: Keep this provider dumb but add an inventory/code-review rule that only approved policy-aware modules call provider send methods.

sendSurfaces.trigger: Historical monolithic scheduler and standalone helper functions retained for compatibility/listing only.
sendSurfaces.risk: Contains old direct Graph send/reply helpers that would be unsafe if reactivated.
sendSurfaces.nextGate: Delete or quarantine direct legacy Graph send helpers after proving no production workflow imports them.

sendSurfaces.trigger: Standalone historical script for sign-in/email-to-Excel flow.
sendSurfaces.risk: Not part of the production scheduler, but direct Graph send/reply calls should not be confused with approved SiteSift send paths.
currentControls: not part of GitHub Actions scheduler
sendSurfaces.nextGate: Move to an archived scripts folder or add a runtime guard before any future cleanup broadens scheduler entrypoints.
